betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Station Docking Port§f is a §6single block§f placed on a deployed space station to allow rockets to dock at the station and refuel. It is the §6station-side counterpart§f to the §6Docking Pad§f (10023) on the planet surface and pairs with a §6Satellite Bay§f (advancedrocketry:loader:1) for full docking functionality.

§6Production Path (stationmarker.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shapeless§f
- Input: 1x §6itemBattery§f (forge:ore_dict), 1x §6Tracking Circuit§f (advancedrocketry:ic:1), 1x §6Satellite Bay§f (advancedrocketry:loader:1)
- Output: 1x §6Station Docking Port§f (advancedrocketry:stationmarker)

The docking port binds to a §6Space Station ID Chip§f when placed on the station. Rockets launched with the same chip will automatically home in on the port and dock, transferring fuel and crew. The port has a 3×3 air clearance requirement like other AR pads.

The docking port is a prerequisite for the §6Warp Travel§f (10038) chain and the §6Station Gravity Controller§f (10037). It is also the entry point for any §6orbital mining§f mission because the rocket must dock at the station to drop off ore.

§eTip§f: Place the docking port on the §6outer hull§f of the station with a §3×3 air column§f in front. Multiple docking ports can be placed on the same station but each port must be bound to a unique §6Space Station ID Chip§f — duplicate bindings will cause the rockets to dock randomly.
